name: Carnival (Rio de Janeiro)
traditions: Samba school parades at the Sambadrome; elaborate costumes and floats; street parties (blocos); music and dancing; masquerade balls.
historicalBackground: Rio's Carnival evolved from Portuguese Entrudo celebrations and African cultural traditions brought by enslaved people. Samba schools emerged in the early 20th century.
description: The world's largest carnival celebration, featuring spectacular samba parades, elaborate costumes, and non-stop music in Rio de Janeiro.
